meta.name: SLR-instructions
meta.description: Instructions and rules for Systematic Literature Review in write-paper skill. Governs how to search, verify, and integrate references.

philosophy: Query → Verify → Integrate → Cross-check. Never cite what you haven't verified. SSoT = PDF→MD files.

source_hierarchy.description: Trust levels for citation sources. Higher = more trusted.

levels.use: Final cross-check. Exact quotes. rg verification.
Latest papers. Real-time search. Gap filling.
Hypothesis only. MUST verify with sources 1-4.

search_workflow.description: Mandatory steps when searching for new references.
steps: 1. Query MCP NewRAG with SHORT keywords (2-4 words). Min 3 queries per topic.
2. Query MCP Perplexity for latest papers (2019-2026). Structured RCIFENI-O format.
3. rg search in SLR/pdfs-md/ for specific terms, authors, or findings.
5. If source unknown: Use APA7 verify workflow (.fong/instructions/academic/instructions-apa7-citation-verify.json).
rules: NEVER cite a paper you haven't verified in at least 2 sources.
NEVER fabricate author names, years, or journal names.
RAG output = hypothesis. Must verify with SSoT (pdfs-md) or abstracts.json.
Perplexity output = hypothesis. Must cross-check before citing.
When in doubt, mark as [UNVERIFIED] and flag for manual review.

mcp_newrag_workflow.description: How to use MCP NewRAG (DKM Knowledge Base) for citation discovery. ~10,000 books.
mcp_newrag_workflow.capacity: ~10,000 books covering TAM, education, Bayesian statistics, research methods

query_strategy.length: SHORT (2-4 words per query). Long queries = poor results.
query_strategy.frequency: Min 3 queries per topic. 7+ queries for complex papers.

output_handling.summary: AI-generated synthesis. Use for understanding, not citation.
output_handling.chunks: Actual text from source PDFs. Contains page numbers.
output_handling.apa7_recommended: AI-inferred citation. MUST verify with hash lookup.
output_handling.score: Relevance score 0-10. Prefer chunks with score >= 7.

verification_workflow: 2. Check if paper already in SLR/abstracts/slr-papers-selected.json
3. If not in SLR: Use APA7 verify workflow to confirm citation
4. Add verified citation to references.bib with full BibTeX
5. Integrate into paper section with context (sample size, method, finding)

hash_lookup.description: Get correct APA7 citation from DKM-PDFs metadata

anti_patterns: NEVER use apa7_recommended directly without verification
NEVER query with long sentences (>6 words)
NEVER skip cross-check with SLR/pdfs-md/ or abstracts.json
NEVER assume NewRAG chunk = complete paper context

pdf_conversion.description: Rules for converting SLR PDFs to searchable text.
rules: ALL PDFs in SLR/pdfs/ MUST be converted to SLR/pdfs-md/ before rg search.
Convert once. Re-convert only if PDF updated.
Naming: same as PDF but .md extension.
Check conversion: wc -l on .md file. 0 lines = failed conversion.

integration_rules.description: Rules for integrating new references into the paper.
bib: Add to LaTeX/bib/references.bib with full BibTeX entry.
Required fields: author, title, journal/publisher, year.
Optional but preferred: volume, number, pages, doi.
Group new entries under comment header: % === NEW REFERENCES (round N) ===
sections: Every claim MUST have a citation. No uncited statements.
Use \citep{} for parenthetical, \citet{} for textual citations.
When adding new citation, also add context (sample size, method, key finding).
Prefer recent papers (2019-2026) over older ones for empirical claims.
build: After adding refs: bibtex + 2x xelatex to resolve all citations.
Check for undefined citation warnings in build log.
Verify page count hasn't dropped (sign of broken LaTeX).

quality_filters.description: Minimum criteria for including a paper as reference.
required: Relevance: TAM, LMS, Moodle, GWfE, Bayesian education, or comparative studies.
Language: English or Vietnamese with English abstract.
preferred: Indexed in Scopus, WoS, or Google Scholar

anti_patterns: NEVER add citation to bib without also using it in at least one section.
NEVER cite paper based solely on AI knowledge without verification.
NEVER use generic team names as authors (e.g., 'Research Group') for real papers.
NEVER skip bibtex rebuild after adding new references.
NEVER assume Perplexity/RAG citations are correct without cross-check.

crossref_api_workflow.description: Verify citation metadata via CrossRef DOI API. Official DOI registry. Very high trust.
crossref_api_workflow.command: curl -s 'https://api.crossref.org/works/{DOI}' | jq '{title: .message.title[0], author: [.message.author[].family], journal: .message["container-title"][0], year: .message.issued["date-parts"][0][0], doi: .message.DOI}'

result.title: The acceptance of Moodle technology by business administration students
author: Gacar

use_cases: Verify DOI exists and resolves correctly
workflow: 3. Compare returned metadata with claimed citation
5. If mismatch: Flag for manual review
anti_patterns: NEVER trust DOI without API verification
NEVER assume DOI format is correct (validate response)
NEVER skip CrossRef when DOI is available
